130003:
Hitori Gotou, nicknamed Bocchi-chan, is a painfully shy high schooler who has spent years teaching herself guitar alone in her room. She's discovered by drummer Nijika Ijichi and drafted into Kessoku Band as their missing guitarist, which means she now has to perform at a live house in Shimokitazawa in front of actual people. The rest of the band is Ryo Yamada on bass and Ikuyo Kita on vocals.

The series follows the four of them as they practice, deal with low turnout, pick up part-time jobs at the venue, and slowly try to build an audience.
21519:
Taki Tachibana is a high school boy living in Tokyo; Mitsuha Miyamizu is a high school girl living in a small mountain town. They begin inexplicably swapping bodies — waking up in each other's lives with no warning — and start leaving notes and instructions for each other to get through the day. Over time they develop feelings for someone they have never actually met in person.

When the swaps suddenly stop, Taki tries to find Mitsuha and discovers that the situation between them is more complicated than he understood. The second half of the film follows him trying to track her down.
120377:
David Martinez is a teenager in Night City whose mother is killed in a shootout, leaving him broke and with nowhere to go. He gets his hands on a piece of military-grade cyberware and installs it, which gets him noticed by a crew of mercenaries called edgerunners. He joins them and starts working his way up, eventually becoming a respected mercenary himself alongside a netrunner named Lucy.

As David takes on more and more cyberware to become stronger, the mental strain of carrying so many implants becomes harder to ignore. The series runs ten episodes.
150672:
A gynecologist named Goro Amemiya is killed on the night his patient — an idol named AI Hoshino who had been secretly pregnant — gives birth. Both of them are reincarnated: Goro becomes AI's newborn son Aquamarine, and his teenage patient Sarina is reborn as twin sister Ruby. They grow up with memories of their past lives intact, watching AI rise to become one of Japan's biggest idols.

When AI is murdered by a stalker, Aqua enters the entertainment industry with the goal of finding the person responsible. Ruby pursues a career as an idol herself. The two storylines run in parallel across the series.
129201:
Shinpei Ajiro returns to his hometown island of Hitogashima for the funeral of his childhood friend Ushio Kofune, whose drowning death was ruled an accident. He suspects something is wrong, and that night learns the island is being infiltrated by creatures called Shadows — entities that create perfect physical copies of islanders and kill the originals. Shinpei is killed, then wakes up at the moment he first arrived on the island with all his memories of what happened intact.

The series follows Shinpei across multiple loops as he uses what he learned each time to gather more information, figure out where the Shadows come from, and try to save the people on the island.
171018:
Momo Ayase and Ken Takakura, who goes by Okarun, are classmates who disagree about the supernatural — Momo believes in ghosts, Okarun believes in aliens. They dare each other to visit a location tied to the other's belief. Momo goes to a UFO hotspot; Okarun goes to a haunted building. Both immediately run into something real. Okarun is abducted by aliens; Momo channels the spirit of a deceased fighter to escape a ghost. They both survive and end up with supernatural abilities.

The series follows them dealing with a series of increasingly powerful paranormal and extraterrestrial threats together. A romance develops between them across the series.
184951:
Miyu Suzuki is an outgoing high school girl who tends to go along with whatever her social circle is doing. Yuusuke Tani is a quiet classmate who says exactly what he thinks and doesn't particularly care what anyone thinks of it. Miyu starts asking him personal questions out of curiosity and finds herself spending more time around him.

The series follows the two of them as their relationship slowly develops, alongside a group of friends who have their own relationships overlapping in the background.
101291:
Sakuta Azusagawa is a high school student who encounters a phenomenon called Adolescence Syndrome — strange, impossible events that happen to specific people as a result of emotional stress. His first case is Mai Sakurajima, a well-known actress who has become completely invisible to everyone around her except Sakuta. The two start dating and end up investigating other cases of the syndrome together as it begins affecting other people in Sakuta's life, including his younger sister.

Each arc focuses on a different person and a different form the syndrome takes. The series is followed by a film, Rascal Does Not Dream of a Dreaming Girl, which continues the story.
166794:
Yuki Itose is a deaf college student who communicates through lip-reading and sign language. On a crowded commuter train she runs into a situation she can't handle alone, and Itsuomi Nagi steps in to help her. He speaks multiple languages and becomes curious about sign language and about Yuki specifically. The two start spending time together and gradually become closer.

The series follows their relationship developing over the course of the school year, along with a secondary couple made up of their respective friends.
21875:
Set six thousand years before the events of No Game, No Life, the world is in the middle of a war between eighteen sentient races that has gone on long enough to begin destroying the planet itself. Riku is a human who has survived by avoiding direct conflict and studying the patterns of the war. He comes across Schwi, a deactivated unit from one of the machine races who has been alone for decades, and the two form a partnership — she provides him with processing power and tactical analysis; he tries to help her understand what having emotions actually means.

Together they work toward a plan to end the war. The film covers the events that led to the world being restructured around the rules seen in the main series.
20872:
Giftias are androids indistinguishable from humans in appearance and personality, used as companions and family members — but they have a hard operational limit of just under eighty-two thousand hours, roughly nine years, after which they lose all memory and personality and become dangerous. Tsukasa Mizugaki joins SAI Corporation's Terminal Service, the division responsible for locating and retrieving Giftias before their time runs out. He's paired with Isla, an experienced Giftia retrieval agent who is herself close to her own expiration.

The series follows Tsukasa and Isla carrying out retrievals together and slowly developing feelings for each other, with Isla's own deadline in the background.
162780:
Utena Hiiragi has been a fan of magical girls since she was a child. When a mysterious creature approaches her with the chance to become one, she agrees — but instead of becoming a hero, she's recruited into the villain organization Enormeeta and sent to fight against the magical girls she grew up admiring. She discovers during her first battle that she has a sadistic side that activates when she's in the villain role.

The series follows Utena and the rest of Enormeeta as they repeatedly clash with the magical girl team. It contains explicit content.
